skill.name: knowledge-base
skill.description: Builds a structured Markdown knowledge base from project concepts, guides, decisions, glossary entries, and source tree discovery.
skill.summary: Create and maintain project knowledge bases with templates, indexes, glossary generation, search, and codebase discovery.
skill.author: Dwsy

security_audit.summary: Static analysis produced many alerts, but most command, network, hidden-file, entropy, and weak-crypto detections are documentation examples or template literals. One confirmed issue remains: decision creation uses an unsanitized title in a filesystem path, allowing path traversal writes inside the project. No prompt injection attempt or data exfiltration behavior was found.

high_findings.title: Path Traversal in Decision Document Creation
high_findings.description: The decision creation path builds the output filename from the user supplied decision title without the same sanitization used for concept and guide names. A crafted title containing path separators and parent directory segments can cause writes outside the intended decisions directory.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code uses the raw decision name after only replacing whitespace, then joins it into a write path. This is a direct filesystem write issue with a clear traversal vector.

medium_findings.title: Broad Project File Read and Report Write Capability
medium_findings.description: The scan and discover commands recursively inspect project directories and write generated Markdown reports under docs/knowledge. This is expected behavior for the skill, but users should understand that running it reads local source files and creates documentation files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file reads and writes are visible and align with the documented feature set. The risk is operational rather than malicious because writes target the knowledge base.

low_findings.title: Unused Child Process Import
low_findings.description: The file imports child_process and creates execAsync, but no call site uses it. This confirms the static external command alert at import time, but no command execution path was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The import is present, but targeted review found no execAsync usage. The finding should be cleaned up, but it is not active command execution.

low_findings.title: Documentation Command Examples Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Many static external command and hidden home directory alerts come from README.md and SKILL.md command examples that show how to run the skill. They are not automatically executed by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are fenced command examples or documentation tables. They describe manual use and do not create an execution path.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Reference Links
low_findings.description: The network findings are Markdown links to related projects and public reference material. No code path performs network requests or sends local data to those URLs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear only in documentation. Review found no fetch, HTTP client, or network execution code.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Terminology False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts map to terms and documentation text such as Token, OAuth, JWT, MD file extensions, and Markdown examples. No cryptographic implementation was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed code contains no hashing, encryption, signing, or verification logic. The alerts are caused by words in domain lists and Markdown structure.

dangerous_patterns.title: Unsanitized User Input in Filesystem Path
dangerous_patterns.description: The create decision command uses a user controlled title to build a path passed to writeFile. This should normalize and validate the final path remains under docs/knowledge/decisions.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The unsafe path construction and subsequent write are directly adjacent in the reviewed code. The risk is confirmed without needing inferred behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Dormant Shell Execution Capability
dangerous_patterns.description: child_process.exec is imported and promisified but unused. It should be removed to reduce future command execution risk and static audit noise.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is present, but it is dormant because no call site was found. The confidence reflects confirmed code presence but no active exploit path.

content.value_statement: Project knowledge often lives in code, chat, and memory, which slows onboarding and reviews. This skill creates structured Markdown concepts, guides, decisions, indexes, and glossaries.
actual_capabilities: Initializes a docs/knowledge folder with concepts, guides, decisions, and external reference sections.
Creates Markdown concept, guide, decision, and term files from included templates.
Scans source files for class, interface, type, enum, and struct names to suggest concepts.
Generates an index from Markdown files in the knowledge base.
Builds a glossary by extracting definition text from concept documents.
Discovers common technical directories and suggests missing knowledge documents.
limitations: Requires Bun or a compatible TypeScript runtime to run lib.ts commands.
Generated content is template-based and still needs human review.
Code scanning uses simple heuristics and may miss domain terms.
Decision titles have a path validation issue and should be fixed before publication.

use_cases.title: Onboard a New Engineer
use_cases.description: Create a shared glossary and concept map before a new teammate starts reading implementation details.

use_cases.title: Document Architecture Decisions
use_cases.description: Record why a team chose a design, which options were rejected, and what tradeoffs remain.

use_cases.title: Turn Code Structure Into Documentation Tasks
use_cases.description: Scan a repository for technical directories and generate a checklist of missing concept and guide pages.

prompt_templates.title: Initialize Documentation
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for an existing repository.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the knowledge-base skill to initialize docs/knowledge for this project. Then explain the created folders and the next two commands I should run.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Concept Entry
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a concept document for [term] in category [category]. Include a clear definition, context, misconceptions, relationships, and references.

prompt_templates.title: Generate Discovery Checklist
prompt_templates.scenario: Find missing documentation from project structure.
prompt_templates.prompt: Run knowledge-base discovery for this repository. Summarize the highest confidence documentation gaps and recommend the first five pages to create.

prompt_templates.title: Maintain a Mature Knowledge Base
prompt_templates.prompt: Scan for new concepts, update the glossary and index, compare the discovery report with existing docs, and propose a documentation maintenance plan.

output_examples.input: Initialize a knowledge base for a backend service.
output_examples.output: Creates concepts, guides, decisions, external, and index files under docs/knowledge. Provides next steps for scanning and creating priority entries.

output_examples.input: Create a concept for Session Token in the auth category.
output_examples.output: Produces a Markdown concept page with definition, context, implementation references, misconceptions, related concepts, and reference placeholders.

output_examples.input: Discover missing documentation in a repository.
output_examples.output: Produces a discovery report with detected technical directories, suggested concepts, suggested guides, confidence levels, and progress tracking.

best_practices: Run discovery before creating many pages, so the folder structure follows the actual repository.
Keep each concept definition short and link deeper explanations to guides or decisions.
Regenerate the index and glossary after adding or moving documents.
anti_patterns: Do not treat generated templates as final documentation without review.
Do not use deeply nested categories unless the project structure requires them.
Do not create decision entries with untrusted or unsanitized titles until the path issue is fixed.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates Markdown knowledge base folders, concept pages, guide pages, decision records, indexes, glossaries, and discovery reports.

faq.question: Does it send project data to external services?
faq.answer: No evidence found. The reviewed code does not contain network request logic.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report marks it as compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does the skill execute shell commands?
faq.answer: The code imports child_process.exec but does not use it. Users still need to run the Bun commands manually.

faq.question: Can it replace human documentation review?
faq.answer: No. It creates structure and suggestions, but teams should verify accuracy and add missing context.

faq.question: Is it ready for marketplace publication?
faq.answer: Not yet. The decision document path validation issue should be fixed before publishing.
